A1→B1 Work / Social ~55s

Catching Up with a Coworker

100Ls Shadowing Material · Lesson 03  ·  ← 上一课
📄 Full Script — Dialogue
Alex — Brad (voice A)
Jamie — Eric (voice B)
Alex
Hey, long time no see! How have you been? I feel like I haven't seen you in forever.
Jamie
I know, right? Things have been pretty crazy on my end. We just wrapped up a big project last week — I think I was in the office until nine every night.
Alex
Oh wow, that sounds exhausting. Are you finally getting some rest?
Jamie
A little bit, yeah. How about you? Anything exciting going on?
Alex
Not too much, honestly. Just the usual. Oh — but I did start going to the gym again.
Jamie
Nice! How's that going?
Alex
It's going well, actually. I'm only going like three times a week, but it makes a big difference.
Jamie
I should really do the same. I keep saying I will and then I never do.
Alex
Ha, I know the feeling. You just have to start — that's the hardest part.
Jamie
True. Anyway, we should grab lunch sometime and catch up properly.
Alex
Yeah, definitely. Let's actually make it happen this time.
Alex：嘿，好久不见！你最近怎么样？感觉好久没见到你了。
Jamie：是啊！我这边最近挺忙的。我们刚完成了一个大项目——上周我几乎每晚都待到九点。
Alex：哇，听起来很累。你终于可以休息一下了吗？
Jamie：稍微休息了一点。你呢？最近有什么新鲜事吗？
Alex：没什么特别的，老样子。哦——不过我又开始去健身房了。
Jamie：不错！进展怎么样？
Alex：其实还不错，我一周只去三次，但感觉差别很大。
Jamie：我也该这样做了。我一直说要去，但就是没行动。
Alex：哈，我懂那种感觉。你只需要开始——那才是最难的部分。
Jamie：说得对。总之，我们应该找个时间一起吃顿午饭，好好叙叙旧。
Alex：是啊，一定要。这次我们真的要说到做到。
今天听了 0 遍
🎯 Sentence Practice
Alex
Hey, long time no see! How have you been?
嘿，好久不见！你最近怎么样？
Jamie
We just wrapped up a big project last week — I think I was in the office until nine every night.
我们刚刚完成了一个大项目——上周我几乎每晚都待到九点。
Alex
I'm only going like three times a week, but it makes a big difference.
我一周只去三次，但感觉差别很大。
Alex
You just have to start — that's the hardest part.
你只需要开始——那才是最难的部分。
Jamie
We should grab lunch sometime and catch up properly.
我们应该找个时间一起吃顿午饭，好好叙叙旧。
Alex
Let's actually make it happen this time.
这次我们真的要说到做到。
📚 Key Vocabulary
long time no see
/lɒŋ taɪm nəʊ siː/
好久不见（非正式问候语）
"Hey, long time no see! How have you been?"
wrap up
/ræp ʌp/
完成，收尾
"We just wrapped up a big project last week."
on my end
/ɒn maɪ end/
在我这边，就我而言
"Things have been pretty crazy on my end."
make a difference
/meɪk ə ˈdɪfrəns/
有影响，有差别
"Going regularly makes a big difference."
catch up (with sb)
/kætʃ ʌp/
叙旧，聊近况
"We should grab lunch and catch up properly."
make it happen
/meɪk ɪt ˈhæpən/
说到做到，让它成真
"Let's actually make it happen this time."
🔗 Connected Speech
How have you been?
/haʊ həv jʊ biːn/
弱读 Reduction
have 在口语中弱读为 /həv/，整句几乎连成一口气。 注意区分 "How ARE you" vs "How HAVE you been" — 后者问的是这段时间的状态。
just wrapped up
/dʒʌs ræpt ʌp/
连读 Linking
just 末尾的 /t/ 与 wrapped 首音连读，听起来像 "jus-rapped-up"。 类似：just arrived, just about
going to the gym
/ˈɡəʊɪŋ tə ðə dʒɪm/
弱读 Reduction
to the 弱读为 /tə ðə/，是日常口语最常见的弱化组合之一。 go to the → "go tə thə"（几乎听不出 "to the"）
make it happen
/ˈmeɪk ɪt ˈhæpən/
连读 Linking
make it 连读紧密，/k/ 和 /ɪt/ 之间几乎没有停顿，听起来像 "maykit"。 固定搭配：Let's make it happen / make it work / make it count
